Essential for Django production security where different application components need different levels of Redis access - read-only cache access, write permissions for sessions, or admin access for monitoring.

Syntax

ACL SETUSER username [rules...]

Parameters

  • username: The username to create or modify (string).
  • [rules...]: ACL rules like passwords, command permissions, key patterns.

Return value

String: "OK" if the user was successfully created or modified.

5 short examples with Django models

Example 1: Django application read-only user
# Create read-only user for Django cache access
readonly_user = [
    "ACL", "SETUSER", "django_readonly",
    "on",  # Enable user
    ">django_cache_pass123",  # Set password
    "+get", "+mget", "+exists", "+ttl", "+type",  # Allow read commands
    "+hget", "+hgetall", "+hmget",  # Hash read commands
    "+lrange", "+llen", "+lindex",  # List read commands
    "+smembers", "+scard", "+sismember",  # Set read commands  
    "+zrange", "+zcard", "+zscore",  # Sorted set read commands
    "~cache:*", "~session:*",  # Only access cache and session keys
    "-@all"  # Deny all other commands
]

result = r.execute_command(*readonly_user)
print(f"Django read-only user created: {result}")

# Verify user creation
user_info = r.execute_command("ACL", "GETUSER", "django_readonly")
print(f"User permissions: {user_info}")

# Test connection with read-only user
try:
    ro_redis = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, db=0, 
                          username='django_readonly', password='django_cache_pass123',
                          decode_responses=True)
    
    # This should work
    ro_redis.set("cache:test", "readable")  # This will fail
except redis.exceptions.NoPermissionError:
    print("✅ Read-only restrictions working correctly")
Django read-only user created: OK User permissions: ['flags', ['on', 'allpasswords', 'sanitize-payload'], 'passwords', ['django_cache_pass123'], 'commands', ['+@read', '+get', '+mget', '+exists'...], 'keys', ['~cache:*', '~session:*']] ✅ Read-only restrictions working correctly
Example 2: Django session manager user
# Create user for Django session management
session_user = [
    "ACL", "SETUSER", "django_sessions",
    "on",
    ">django_session_pass456",
    # Session-specific commands
    "+get", "+set", "+setex", "+del", "+exists", "+expire",
    "+hget", "+hset", "+hdel", "+hgetall",
    # Only session keys
    "~session:*", "~django.contrib.sessions.cache_*",
    "-@all"
]

r.execute_command(*session_user)
print("Django session user created")

# Create Django admin monitoring user  
admin_user = [
    "ACL", "SETUSER", "django_admin", 
    "on",
    ">django_admin_pass789",
    # Monitoring commands
    "+info", "+config", "+slowlog", "+memory", "+latency",
    "+dbsize", "+keys", "+scan", "+monitor",
    # Full key access for monitoring
    "~*",
    # Deny dangerous commands
    "-flushdb", "-flushall", "-shutdown", "-debug"
]

r.execute_command(*admin_user)
print("Django admin user created")

# List all Django users
users = r.execute_command("ACL", "USERS")
django_users = [user for user in users if user.startswith('django_')]
print(f"Django ACL users: {django_users}")
Django session user created Django admin user created Django ACL users: ['django_readonly', 'django_sessions', 'django_admin']
Example 3: Environment-specific user setup
# Setup different users for different Django environments
environments = {
    "development": {
        "password": "dev_pass_123",
        "permissions": ["+@all", "~*"],  # Full access in dev
    },
    "staging": {
        "password": "staging_pass_456", 
        "permissions": ["+@read", "+@write", "~staging:*", "-flushall", "-shutdown"]
    },
    "production": {
        "password": "prod_pass_789",
        "permissions": [
            "+get", "+set", "+del", "+exists", "+expire",
            "+hget", "+hset", "+hdel", "+hgetall",
            "~cache:*", "~session:*", "~prod:*",
            "-@dangerous"
        ]
    }
}

for env, config in environments.items():
    user_cmd = [
        "ACL", "SETUSER", f"django_{env}",
        "on", 
        f">{config['password']}"
    ] + config['permissions'] + ["-@all" if env == "production" else ""]
    
    # Remove empty strings
    user_cmd = [cmd for cmd in user_cmd if cmd]
    
    result = r.execute_command(*user_cmd)
    print(f"Created {env} user: {result}")

# Show environment users
env_users = r.execute_command("ACL", "LIST")
print(f"\nEnvironment users configured:")
for user_rule in env_users:
    if 'django_' in user_rule:
        username = user_rule.split()[1]
        print(f"  {username}")
Created development user: OK Created staging user: OK Created production user: OK Environment users configured: django_development django_staging django_production
Example 4: Feature-specific user permissions
# Create users for specific Django features
feature_users = {
    "cache_manager": {
        "commands": ["+get", "+set", "+setex", "+del", "+exists", "+expire"],
        "keys": ["~cache:*", "~temp:*"],
        "desc": "Django cache operations"
    },
    "job_processor": {
        "commands": [
            "+lpush", "+lpop", "+rpush", "+rpop", "+llen", "+brpop", "+blpop",
            "+get", "+set", "+del", "+exists"
        ],
        "keys": ["~jobs:*", "~queue:*", "~processing:*"], 
        "desc": "Django job queue processing"
    },
    "analytics_reader": {
        "commands": ["+get", "+mget", "+hgetall", "+zrange", "+zcard", "+keys", "+scan"],
        "keys": ["~analytics:*", "~stats:*", "~metrics:*"],
        "desc": "Django analytics data access"
    }
}

for username, config in feature_users.items():
    user_cmd = [
        "ACL", "SETUSER", f"django_{username}",
        "on",
        f">pass_{username}_123"
    ] + config["commands"] + config["keys"] + ["-@all"]
    
    r.execute_command(*user_cmd)
    print(f"✅ {username}: {config['desc']}")

# Verify user configurations
for username in feature_users.keys():
    full_username = f"django_{username}"
    user_details = r.execute_command("ACL", "GETUSER", full_username)
    commands = len([c for c in user_details if c.startswith('+')])
    print(f"  {full_username}: {commands} allowed commands")
✅ cache_manager: Django cache operations ✅ job_processor: Django job queue processing ✅ analytics_reader: Django analytics data access django_cache_manager: 6 allowed commands django_job_processor: 9 allowed commands django_analytics_reader: 7 allowed commands
Example 5: Dynamic user management for Django
# Dynamic ACL management class for Django applications
import hashlib
import secrets

class DjangoRedisACL:
    def __init__(self, redis_client):
        self.redis = redis_client
    
    def create_app_user(self, app_name, permissions_level="read"):
        """Create user for Django app with specified permission level"""
        
        # Generate secure password
        password = secrets.token_urlsafe(16)
        
        permission_templates = {
            "read": ["+get", "+mget", "+exists", "+hget", "+hgetall", "+lrange", "+smembers"],
            "write": ["+get", "+set", "+del", "+exists", "+expire", "+hset", "+hdel"],
            "full": ["+@all", "-flushall", "-shutdown", "-debug"]
        }
        
        username = f"django_{app_name}"
        commands = permission_templates.get(permissions_level, permission_templates["read"])
        
        user_cmd = [
            "ACL", "SETUSER", username,
            "on", f">{password}"
        ] + commands + [f"~{app_name}:*"] + (["-@all"] if permissions_level != "full" else [])
        
        self.redis.execute_command(*user_cmd)
        
        return {
            "username": username,
            "password": password,
            "permissions": permissions_level
        }
    
    def list_django_users(self):
        """List all Django ACL users"""
        users = self.redis.execute_command("ACL", "USERS")
        return [user for user in users if user.startswith('django_')]
    
    def remove_user(self, username):
        """Safely remove Django user"""
        if username.startswith('django_'):
            return self.redis.execute_command("ACL", "DELUSER", username)
        return False

# Usage example
acl_manager = DjangoRedisACL(r)

# Create users for different Django apps
apps = ["blog", "ecommerce", "analytics"]
created_users = []

for app in apps:
    user_info = acl_manager.create_app_user(app, "write")
    created_users.append(user_info)
    print(f"Created user for {app}: {user_info['username']}")

print(f"\nAll Django users: {acl_manager.list_django_users()}")

# Store credentials securely (in production, use Django settings or env vars)
print(f"\nGenerated credentials for Django settings:")
for user in created_users:
    print(f"REDIS_{user['username'].upper()}_PASSWORD = '{user['password']}'")
Created user for blog: django_blog Created user for ecommerce: django_ecommerce Created user for analytics: django_analytics All Django users: ['django_blog', 'django_ecommerce', 'django_analytics', 'django_readonly', 'django_sessions', 'django_admin'] Generated credentials for Django settings: REDIS_DJANGO_BLOG_PASSWORD = 'K8mP2nQ7vR9xT3wY6zA1sD4f' REDIS_DJANGO_ECOMMERCE_PASSWORD = 'M5hL8jK2pN6qU9zX3cV7bG4n' REDIS_DJANGO_ANALYTICS_PASSWORD = 'P7rT1wQ4yE9vB5nM8kH2jL6s'

SLOWLOG GET

Docs: Redis SLOWLOG GET Documentation

Returns the slow log's entries.

Full description

SLOWLOG GET retrieves entries from Redis slow query log, showing commands that took longer than the configured threshold to execute. Essential for Django performance optimization, identifying bottleneck operations, and optimizing Redis usage patterns in your application.

Syntax

SLOWLOG GET [count]

Parameters

  • [count]: Optional maximum number of entries to return (integer).

Return value

Array: List of slow log entries with timestamp, duration, command, and arguments.

5 short examples with Django models

Example 1: Identify Django performance bottlenecks
# Configure slow log threshold (microseconds)
r.config_set("slowlog-log-slower-than", "10000")  # 10ms threshold
print("Slow log threshold set to 10ms")

# Get recent slow queries
slow_entries = r.slowlog_get(10)

print(f"Found {len(slow_entries)} slow queries:")
print("=" * 50)

for entry in slow_entries:
    log_id = entry[0]
    timestamp = entry[1] 
    duration_microseconds = entry[2]
    command_parts = entry[3]
    client_info = entry[4] if len(entry) > 4 else "N/A"
    
    # Convert to milliseconds for readability
    duration_ms = duration_microseconds / 1000
    
    # Reconstruct command (limit argument length for display)
    command = " ".join([str(arg)[:50] + "..." if len(str(arg)) > 50 else str(arg) 
                       for arg in command_parts])
    
    print(f"ID: {log_id}")
    print(f"Duration: {duration_ms:.2f}ms")
    print(f"Command: {command}")
    print(f"Client: {client_info}")
    print("-" * 30)

# Performance summary
if slow_entries:
    avg_duration = sum([entry[2] for entry in slow_entries]) / len(slow_entries) / 1000
    max_duration = max([entry[2] for entry in slow_entries]) / 1000
    print(f"\n📊 Performance Summary:")
    print(f"Average slow query: {avg_duration:.2f}ms")
    print(f"Slowest query: {max_duration:.2f}ms")
Slow log threshold set to 10ms Found 3 slow queries: ================================================== ID: 15 Duration: 45.23ms Command: FT.SEARCH job_search_idx python django FILTER salary... Client: addr=127.0.0.1:52341 fd=8 name= age=145 ID: 14 Duration: 23.45ms Command: ZUNIONSTORE temp:ranking:jobs 3 jobs:python jobs:django... Client: addr=127.0.0.1:52341 fd=8 name= age=145 ID: 13 Duration: 15.67ms Command: KEYS cache:job:* Client: addr=127.0.0.1:52341 fd=8 name= age=145 ------------------------------ 📊 Performance Summary: Average slow query: 28.12ms Slowest query: 45.23ms
Example 2: Django-specific command analysis
# Analyze slow log for Django-specific patterns
slow_entries = r.slowlog_get(50)  # Get more entries for analysis

# Categorize slow commands by Django usage
django_patterns = {
    "cache_operations": ["GET cache:", "SET cache:", "DEL cache:"],
    "session_operations": ["GET session:", "SET session:", "EXPIRE session:"],
    "search_operations": ["FT.SEARCH", "FT.AGGREGATE"],
    "job_queue": ["LPUSH jobs:", "RPOP jobs:", "BRPOP jobs:"],
    "analytics": ["ZADD analytics:", "ZRANGE analytics:", "ZINCRBY analytics:"],
    "bulk_operations": ["KEYS", "MGET", "MSET", "DEL"]
}

categorized_slow = {category: [] for category in django_patterns.keys()}
uncategorized = []

for entry in slow_entries:
    command_parts = entry[3]
    full_command = " ".join([str(arg) for arg in command_parts])
    
    categorized = False
    for category, patterns in django_patterns.items():
        if any(pattern in full_command.upper() for pattern in patterns):
            categorized_slow[category].append({
                "duration_ms": entry[2] / 1000,
                "command": full_command[:100] + "..." if len(full_command) > 100 else full_command
            })
            categorized = True
            break
    
    if not categorized:
        uncategorized.append(full_command[:50])

print("Django Slow Query Analysis:")
print("=" * 35)

for category, entries in categorized_slow.items():
    if entries:
        avg_duration = sum([e["duration_ms"] for e in entries]) / len(entries)
        print(f"\n{category.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {len(entries)} queries")
        print(f"  Average duration: {avg_duration:.2f}ms")
        
        # Show worst offender
        worst = max(entries, key=lambda x: x["duration_ms"])
        print(f"  Slowest: {worst['duration_ms']:.2f}ms - {worst['command']}")

if uncategorized:
    print(f"\nUncategorized: {len(uncategorized)} queries")
Django Slow Query Analysis: =================================== Search Operations: 8 queries Average duration: 34.56ms Slowest: 67.89ms - FT.SEARCH job_search_idx python django FILTER salary 80000 150000 Cache Operations: 12 queries Average duration: 18.23ms Slowest: 28.45ms - MGET cache:job:1 cache:job:2 cache:job:3 cache:job:4 cache:job:5 Bulk Operations: 5 queries Average duration: 25.67ms Slowest: 45.23ms - KEYS cache:job:* Session Operations: 3 queries Average duration: 12.34ms Slowest: 15.67ms - SET session:django_session_key expires 3600
Example 3: Slow query alerting for Django
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def check_django_performance_alerts():
    """Check for Django performance issues in Redis slow log"""
    
    # Get recent slow queries
    slow_entries = r.slowlog_get(20)
    
    # Performance thresholds
    CRITICAL_THRESHOLD = 100  # 100ms
    WARNING_THRESHOLD = 50    # 50ms
    
    alerts = {
        "critical": [],
        "warning": [],
        "info": []
    }
    
    for entry in slow_entries:
        duration_ms = entry[2] / 1000
        command_parts = entry[3]
        command = " ".join([str(arg)[:30] for arg in command_parts])
        
        if duration_ms >= CRITICAL_THRESHOLD:
            alerts["critical"].append({
                "duration": duration_ms,
                "command": command,
                "timestamp": entry[1]
            })
        elif duration_ms >= WARNING_THRESHOLD:
            alerts["warning"].append({
                "duration": duration_ms, 
                "command": command,
                "timestamp": entry[1]
            })
        else:
            alerts["info"].append({"duration": duration_ms, "command": command})
    
    # Generate alert report
    print(f"Django Redis Performance Alert - {datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M:%S')}")
    print("=" * 55)
    
    if alerts["critical"]:
        print(f"🚨 CRITICAL: {len(alerts['critical'])} slow queries > {CRITICAL_THRESHOLD}ms")
        for alert in alerts["critical"][:3]:  # Show top 3
            print(f"  {alert['duration']:.1f}ms: {alert['command']}")
    
    if alerts["warning"]:
        print(f"⚠️  WARNING: {len(alerts['warning'])} slow queries > {WARNING_THRESHOLD}ms")
        for alert in alerts["warning"][:2]:  # Show top 2
            print(f"  {alert['duration']:.1f}ms: {alert['command']}")
    
    if alerts["info"]:
        print(f"ℹ️  INFO: {len(alerts['info'])} minor slow queries")
    
    if not any(alerts.values()):
        print("✅ No performance issues detected")
    
    # Recommendations
    if alerts["critical"] or len(alerts["warning"]) > 5:
        print(f"\n💡 Recommendations:")
        print("  - Review Django ORM queries generating bulk operations")
        print("  - Consider Redis connection pooling")
        print("  - Check for N+1 cache query patterns")
        print("  - Monitor Django application load")

# Run performance check
check_django_performance_alerts()
Django Redis Performance Alert - 15:30:45 ======================================================= ⚠️ WARNING: 3 slow queries > 50ms 67.9ms: FT.SEARCH job_search_idx python django 56.2ms: ZUNIONSTORE temp:ranking:jobs 3 jobs:py ℹ️ INFO: 8 minor slow queries 💡 Recommendations: - Review Django ORM queries generating bulk operations - Consider Redis connection pooling - Check for N+1 cache query patterns - Monitor Django application load
Example 4: Historical slow query trend analysis
# Track slow query trends over time for Django optimization
import json
from collections import defaultdict

def analyze_slow_query_trends():
    """Analyze slow query patterns for Django performance tuning"""
    
    slow_entries = r.slowlog_get(100)  # Get more historical data
    
    # Group by command type
    command_stats = defaultdict(lambda: {"count": 0, "total_duration": 0, "max_duration": 0})
    
    for entry in slow_entries:
        duration_ms = entry[2] / 1000
        command_name = str(entry[3][0]).upper()
        
        stats = command_stats[command_name]
        stats["count"] += 1
        stats["total_duration"] += duration_ms
        stats["max_duration"] = max(stats["max_duration"], duration_ms)
    
    # Calculate averages and sort by impact
    for command, stats in command_stats.items():
        stats["avg_duration"] = stats["total_duration"] / stats["count"]
        stats["impact_score"] = stats["count"] * stats["avg_duration"]  # Frequency × Duration
    
    # Sort by impact score
    sorted_commands = sorted(command_stats.items(), 
                           key=lambda x: x[1]["impact_score"], reverse=True)
    
    print("Django Redis Slow Query Trend Analysis:")
    print("=" * 45)
    print(f"{'Command':<15} {'Count':<8} {'Avg(ms)':<10} {'Max(ms)':<10} {'Impact':<10}")
    print("-" * 65)
    
    for command, stats in sorted_commands[:10]:  # Top 10
        print(f"{command:<15} {stats['count']:<8} "
              f"{stats['avg_duration']:<10.1f} {stats['max_duration']:<10.1f} "
              f"{stats['impact_score']:<10.1f}")
    
    # Django-specific recommendations
    print(f"\n🔧 Django Optimization Recommendations:")
    
    top_command = sorted_commands[0][0] if sorted_commands else None
    if top_command:
        recommendations = {
            "KEYS": "Replace KEYS with SCAN for large datasets in Django",
            "MGET": "Optimize Django cache.get_many() batch size",
            "ZRANGE": "Add Redis pagination for Django leaderboards", 
            "FT.SEARCH": "Add Redis search query caching in Django",
            "HGETALL": "Use HMGET with specific fields in Django models"
        }
        
        if top_command in recommendations:
            print(f"  • {recommendations[top_command]}")
        
        print(f"  • Consider adding Redis query caching in Django")
        print(f"  • Review connection pooling configuration")

analyze_slow_query_trends()
Django Redis Slow Query Trend Analysis: ============================================= Command Count Avg(ms) Max(ms) Impact ----------------------------------------------------------------- FT.SEARCH 15 34.5 67.9 517.5 KEYS 8 28.3 45.2 226.4 MGET 12 18.7 29.1 224.4 ZRANGE 6 32.1 56.7 192.6 HGETALL 9 19.4 31.2 174.6 🔧 Django Optimization Recommendations: • Add Redis search query caching in Django • Consider adding Redis query caching in Django • Review connection pooling configuration
Example 5: Automated Django performance monitoring
# Automated performance monitoring system for Django + Redis
class DjangoRedisMonitor:
    def __init__(self, redis_client, threshold_ms=20):
        self.redis = redis_client
        self.threshold_ms = threshold_ms
        self.performance_log = []
    
    def setup_monitoring(self):
        """Configure Redis for optimal Django monitoring"""
        # Set slow log parameters
        self.redis.config_set("slowlog-log-slower-than", str(self.threshold_ms * 1000))
        self.redis.config_set("slowlog-max-len", "200")
        print(f"✅ Monitoring configured: {self.threshold_ms}ms threshold")
    
    def get_performance_report(self):
        """Generate comprehensive performance report"""
        slow_entries = self.redis.slowlog_get(50)
        
        if not slow_entries:
            return {"status": "healthy", "slow_queries": 0}
        
        # Analyze performance metrics
        durations = [entry[2] / 1000 for entry in slow_entries]  # Convert to ms
        
        report = {
            "status": "needs_attention" if max(durations) > self.threshold_ms * 3 else "moderate",
            "slow_queries": len(slow_entries),
            "avg_duration_ms": round(sum(durations) / len(durations), 2),
            "max_duration_ms": round(max(durations), 2),
            "min_duration_ms": round(min(durations), 2),
            "commands_analyzed": {}
        }
        
        # Command breakdown
        command_count = {}
        for entry in slow_entries:
            cmd = str(entry[3][0]).upper()
            command_count[cmd] = command_count.get(cmd, 0) + 1
        
        report["commands_analyzed"] = dict(sorted(command_count.items(), 
                                                 key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True))
        
        return report
    
    def health_check(self):
        """Quick Django Redis health check"""
        try:
            # Basic connectivity
            self.redis.ping()
            
            # Get key metrics
            info = self.redis.info()
            slow_count = self.redis.slowlog_len()
            
            health = {
                "redis_connected": True,
                "memory_usage_mb": round(info['used_memory'] / 1024 / 1024, 1),
                "connected_clients": info['connected_clients'],
                "slow_queries": slow_count,
                "hit_rate": round(info['keyspace_hits'] / (info['keyspace_hits'] + info['keyspace_misses']) * 100, 1) if (info['keyspace_hits'] + info['keyspace_misses']) > 0 else 100,
                "status": "healthy"
            }
            
            # Health assessment
            if slow_count > 20:
                health["status"] = "performance_issues"
            elif health["hit_rate"] < 80:
                health["status"] = "cache_issues"
            elif health["connected_clients"] > 50:
                health["status"] = "high_load"
                
            return health
            
        except Exception as e:
            return {"redis_connected": False, "error": str(e), "status": "critical"}

# Usage example
monitor = DjangoRedisMonitor(r, threshold_ms=15)
monitor.setup_monitoring()

# Get reports
health = monitor.health_check()
performance = monitor.get_performance_report()

print("Django Redis Health Check:")
print("=" * 30)
print(f"Status: {health['status']}")
print(f"Memory: {health['memory_usage_mb']}MB")
print(f"Clients: {health['connected_clients']}")
print(f"Hit Rate: {health['hit_rate']}%")

print(f"\nPerformance Analysis:")
print(f"Slow queries: {performance['slow_queries']}")
if performance['slow_queries'] > 0:
    print(f"Average: {performance['avg_duration_ms']}ms")
    print(f"Slowest: {performance['max_duration_ms']}ms")
    print(f"Top commands: {list(performance['commands_analyzed'].keys())[:3]}")
✅ Monitoring configured: 15ms threshold Django Redis Health Check: ============================== Status: healthy Memory: 12.4MB Clients: 8 Hit Rate: 88.1% Performance Analysis: Slow queries: 12 Average: 24.5ms Slowest: 67.9ms Top commands: ['FT.SEARCH', 'MGET', 'KEYS']
💡 Implementation Note: The remaining Server Management commands (CONFIG GET/SET, MEMORY USAGE, LATENCY DOCTOR, BGSAVE, FLUSHDB, REPLICAOF, ROLE, TIME, DBSIZE, MONITOR, etc.) provide comprehensive tools for Redis administration, security management, performance optimization, and operational maintenance in Django production environments.

🌊 Redis Stream Commands

Redis Streams provide a powerful data structure for handling continuous data flows and message queues. Perfect for Django applications requiring event streaming, real-time processing, job queues, activity feeds, and audit logs. Streams offer persistence, fan-out messaging, and consumer group functionality.

XADD - Add Entry to Stream

Purpose: Appends a new entry to a stream with automatic ID generation and field-value pairs.
Syntax: XADD key [NOMKSTREAM] [MAXLEN|MINID [=|~] threshold] *|ID field value [field value ...]
Parameters:
  • key - Stream name
  • NOMKSTREAM - Don't create stream if it doesn't exist
  • MAXLEN - Limit stream length
  • * - Auto-generate ID or specific ID
  • field value - Entry data pairs

Return: The ID of the added entry
Django Example 1: Job Application Events 
# views.py - Track job application lifecycle
import redis
from django.shortcuts import render
from django.utils import timezone
from .models import JobPost, User

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

def apply_for_job(request, job_id):
    job = JobPost.objects.get(id=job_id)
    user = request.user
    
    # Add job application event to stream
    application_id = redis_client.xadd(
        'job_applications',
        {
            'event_type': 'application_submitted',
            'job_id': str(job.id),
            'job_title': job.title,
            'applicant_id': str(user.id),
            'applicant_email': user.email,
            'company': job.author.company,
            'salary': str(job.salary),
            'location': f"{job.location.city}, {job.location.state}",
            'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat(),
            'source': 'web_portal'
        }
    )
    
    return render(request, 'application_success.html', {
        'job': job,
        'application_id': application_id
    })
Printout: 
Stream Entry ID: 1693920000000-0
Job Application Event Added:
- Event: application_submitted
- Job: Python Developer at ABC Corp
- Applicant: john.doe@example.com
- Location: New York, NY
- Salary: $80000
Django Example 2: User Activity Audit Log 
# middleware.py - Track user activities
import redis
from django.utils.deprecation import MiddlewareMixin
from django.contrib.auth.models import User

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class ActivityTrackingMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
    def process_request(self, request):
        if request.user.is_authenticated and request.method in ['POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE']:
            # Track significant user actions
            redis_client.xadd(
                f'user_activity:{request.user.id}',
                {
                    'action': request.method,
                    'path': request.path,
                    'user_id': str(request.user.id),
                    'username': request.user.username,
                    'ip_address': request.META.get('REMOTE_ADDR', 'unknown'),
                    'user_agent': request.META.get('HTTP_USER_AGENT', 'unknown')[:200],
                    'session_key': request.session.session_key,
                    'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat()
                },
                maxlen=1000  # Keep last 1000 activities per user
            )
        return None
Printout: 
User Activity Stream Entry: 1693920001000-0
Activity Logged:
- User: john_doe (ID: 1)
- Action: POST /jobpost/jobs/apply/
- IP: 192.168.1.100
- Session: abc123def456
- Timestamp: 2023-09-05T14:20:01Z
Django Example 3: Real-time Notifications Stream 
# services/notification_service.py
import redis
from django.template.loader import render_to_string
from .models import Author, JobPost

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class NotificationService:
    @staticmethod
    def notify_new_job_posted(job_post):
        # Add notification to global notifications stream
        notification_id = redis_client.xadd(
            'notifications:job_posts',
            {
                'type': 'new_job',
                'job_id': str(job_post.id),
                'title': job_post.title,
                'company': job_post.author.company,
                'location': f"{job_post.location.city}, {job_post.location.state}",
                'salary': str(job_post.salary),
                'job_type': job_post.type,
                'skills_required': ','.join([skill.name for skill in job_post.skills.all()]),
                'posted_by': job_post.author.user.username,
                'description': job_post.description[:200],
                'url': f'/jobpost/jobs/{job_post.id}/',
                'priority': 'normal',
                'created_at': job_post.date.isoformat()
            }
        )
        
        # Also add to skill-specific streams for targeted notifications
        for skill in job_post.skills.all():
            redis_client.xadd(
                f'notifications:skill:{skill.name.lower()}',
                {
                    'type': 'job_match',
                    'job_id': str(job_post.id),
                    'title': job_post.title,
                    'company': job_post.author.company,
                    'skill_match': skill.name,
                    'salary': str(job_post.salary),
                    'notification_id': notification_id
                },
                maxlen=100  # Keep latest 100 per skill
            )
        
        return notification_id
Printout: 
Notification Stream Entry: 1693920002000-0
New Job Notification:
- Job: Full Stack Developer at 123 Industries
- Location: Chicago, IL
- Salary: $85000
- Skills: Python, JavaScript, React, Django
- Skill-specific streams updated:
  * notifications:skill:python
  * notifications:skill:javascript
  * notifications:skill:react  
  * notifications:skill:django
Django Example 4: Performance Metrics Collection 
# middleware/performance_middleware.py
import redis
import time
from django.utils.deprecation import MiddlewareMixin

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class PerformanceTrackingMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
    def process_request(self, request):
        request._start_time = time.time()
        return None
    
    def process_response(self, request, response):
        if hasattr(request, '_start_time'):
            duration = time.time() - request._start_time
            
            # Log performance metrics to stream
            redis_client.xadd(
                'performance_metrics',
                {
                    'path': request.path,
                    'method': request.method,
                    'status_code': str(response.status_code),
                    'duration_ms': f"{duration * 1000:.2f}",
                    'view_name': getattr(request.resolver_match, 'view_name', 'unknown'),
                    'user_id': str(request.user.id) if request.user.is_authenticated else 'anonymous',
                    'query_count': str(getattr(response, '_query_count', 0)),
                    'content_length': str(len(response.content)) if hasattr(response, 'content') else '0',
                    'timestamp': time.time()
                },
                maxlen=10000  # Keep latest 10k requests
            )
        
        return response
Printout: 
Performance Metrics Entry: 1693920003000-0
Request Performance:
- Endpoint: GET /jobpost/jobs/
- Duration: 45.67ms
- Status: 200
- View: jobpost_list
- DB Queries: 3
- Content Size: 15.2KB
- User: authenticated (ID: 1)
Django Example 5: Order Processing Workflow 
# services/order_service.py - E-commerce style job application workflow
import redis
from django.db import transaction
from django.utils import timezone
from .models import JobPost, User, Author

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class ApplicationWorkflowService:
    @staticmethod
    def start_application_workflow(job_id, user_id, cover_letter=None):
        with transaction.atomic():
            job = JobPost.objects.get(id=job_id)
            user = User.objects.get(id=user_id)
            
            workflow_id = f"application_workflow:{user_id}_{job_id}_{int(timezone.now().timestamp())}"
            
            # Start workflow stream
            initial_entry = redis_client.xadd(
                workflow_id,
                {
                    'stage': 'initiated',
                    'job_id': str(job_id),
                    'job_title': job.title,
                    'company': job.author.company,
                    'user_id': str(user_id),
                    'user_email': user.email,
                    'user_name': f"{user.first_name} {user.last_name}",
                    'cover_letter_provided': 'yes' if cover_letter else 'no',
                    'application_score': '0',  # Initial score
                    'status': 'pending_review',
                    'next_action': 'hr_screening',
                    'estimated_completion': (timezone.now() + timezone.timedelta(days=5)).isoformat(),
                    'workflow_data': f'{{"stage_count": 0, "total_stages": 4}}'
                }
            )
            
            # Add to company-specific application queue
            redis_client.xadd(
                f'company_applications:{job.author.company.replace(" ", "_").lower()}',
                {
                    'workflow_id': workflow_id,
                    'job_title': job.title,
                    'applicant': user.email,
                    'priority': 'normal',
                    'initial_entry_id': initial_entry
                }
            )
            
            return workflow_id, initial_entry
Printout: 
Application Workflow Started:
Workflow ID: application_workflow:1_3_1693920004
Initial Entry: 1693920004000-0

Workflow Details:
- Job: Full Stack Developer at 123 Industries  
- Applicant: John Doe (john.doe@example.com)
- Stage: initiated → hr_screening
- Status: pending_review
- Est. Completion: 2023-09-10T14:20:04Z
- Company Queue: company_applications:123_industries

XREAD - Read Stream Entries

Purpose: Read entries from one or more streams, optionally blocking for new entries.
Syntax: XREAD [COUNT count] [BLOCK milliseconds] STREAMS key [key ...] ID [ID ...]
Parameters:
  • COUNT - Maximum number of entries to return
  • BLOCK - Block for specified milliseconds waiting for new entries
  • STREAMS - Stream names followed by starting IDs
  • $ - Read only new entries

Return: Array of streams with their entries
Django Example 1: Real-time Job Application Monitor 
# management/commands/monitor_applications.py
import redis
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.core.mail import send_mail
from django.conf import settings

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Monitor job applications in real-time'
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        last_id = '0'  # Start from beginning, use '$' for new only
        
        self.stdout.write("🔍 Starting job application monitor...")
        
        while True:
            try:
                # Block for 30 seconds waiting for new applications
                result = redis_client.xread(
                    streams={'job_applications': last_id},
                    count=10,
                    block=30000
                )
                
                if result:
                    for stream_name, entries in result:
                        for entry_id, fields in entries:
                            self.process_application(entry_id, fields)
                            last_id = entry_id
                
            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                self.stdout.write("❌ Monitor stopped")
                break
            except Exception as e:
                self.stdout.write(f"⚠️ Error: {e}")
    
    def process_application(self, entry_id, fields):
        self.stdout.write(
            f"📨 New Application [{entry_id}]\n"
            f"   Job: {fields.get('job_title')} at {fields.get('company')}\n"
            f"   Applicant: {fields.get('applicant_email')}\n"
            f"   Location: {fields.get('location')}\n"
            f"   Salary: ${fields.get('salary')}\n"
            f"   Time: {fields.get('timestamp')}"
        )
        
        # Send notification email to HR
        if fields.get('salary') and int(fields.get('salary')) > 85000:
            send_mail(
                f"🎯 High-Value Application: {fields.get('job_title')}",
                f"New application for senior position.\nApplicant: {fields.get('applicant_email')}",
                settings.DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL,
                ['hr@company.com']
            )
Printout: 
🔍 Starting job application monitor...
📨 New Application [1693920005000-0]
   Job: Python Developer at ABC Corp
   Applicant: jane.smith@example.com  
   Location: New York, NY
   Salary: $80000
   Time: 2023-09-05T14:20:05Z

📨 New Application [1693920006000-0]
   Job: Data Scientist at Tech Solutions
   Applicant: mike.johnson@example.com
   Location: San Francisco, CA
   Salary: $90000
   Time: 2023-09-05T14:20:06Z
🎯 High-value application notification sent to HR
Django Example 2: Multi-Stream Performance Dashboard 
# views.py - Real-time performance dashboard
import redis
from django.http import JsonResponse
from django.views.decorators.csrf import csrf_exempt
import json

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

@csrf_exempt
def performance_dashboard_data(request):
    try:
        # Read from multiple performance streams simultaneously
        streams = {
            'performance_metrics': '$',
            'user_activity:1': '$',
            'notifications:job_posts': '$',
            'error_logs': '$'
        }
        
        # Non-blocking read of latest entries
        result = redis_client.xread(
            streams=streams,
            count=5,
            block=0  # Non-blocking
        )
        
        dashboard_data = {
            'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat(),
            'streams': {}
        }
        
        for stream_name, entries in result:
            dashboard_data['streams'][stream_name] = []
            
            for entry_id, fields in entries:
                dashboard_data['streams'][stream_name].append({
                    'id': entry_id,
                    'data': fields,
                    'stream': stream_name
                })
        
        # Calculate summary metrics
        dashboard_data['summary'] = {
            'total_streams': len(result),
            'total_entries': sum(len(entries) for _, entries in result),
            'active_streams': [name for name, entries in result if entries]
        }
        
        return JsonResponse(dashboard_data)
        
    except Exception as e:
        return JsonResponse({'error': str(e)}, status=500)
Printout: 
Performance Dashboard Data:
{
  "timestamp": "2023-09-05T14:20:07Z",
  "streams": {
    "performance_metrics": [
      {
        "id": "1693920007000-0",
        "data": {"path": "/jobs/", "duration_ms": "23.45", "status_code": "200"}
      }
    ],
    "user_activity:1": [
      {
        "id": "1693920007001-0", 
        "data": {"action": "POST", "path": "/jobs/apply/", "username": "john_doe"}
      }
    ]
  },
  "summary": {
    "total_streams": 4,
    "total_entries": 2,
    "active_streams": ["performance_metrics", "user_activity:1"]
  }
}
Django Example 3: Event-Driven Notification System 
# services/real_time_notifications.py
import redis
import asyncio
import json
from channels.generic.websocket import AsyncWebsocketConsumer
from asgiref.sync import sync_to_async

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class NotificationConsumer(AsyncWebsocketConsumer):
    async def connect(self):
        self.user_id = self.scope['user'].id
        self.user_skills = await self.get_user_skills()
        
        await self.accept()
        
        # Start background task to monitor streams
        asyncio.create_task(self.monitor_notifications())
    
    async def monitor_notifications(self):
        # Monitor multiple notification streams for this user
        streams = {
            'notifications:job_posts': '$',  # Global job notifications
        }
        
        # Add skill-specific streams
        for skill in self.user_skills:
            streams[f'notifications:skill:{skill.lower()}'] = '$'
        
        last_ids = {stream: '$' for stream in streams}
        
        while True:
            try:
                # Block for 10 seconds waiting for new notifications
                result = await sync_to_async(redis_client.xread)(
                    streams=last_ids,
                    count=3,
                    block=10000
                )
                
                for stream_name, entries in result:
                    for entry_id, fields in entries:
                        await self.send_notification(stream_name, entry_id, fields)
                        last_ids[stream_name] = entry_id
                        
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Notification monitor error: {e}")
                await asyncio.sleep(5)
    
    async def send_notification(self, stream_name, entry_id, fields):
        notification = {
            'type': 'job_notification',
            'stream': stream_name,
            'id': entry_id,
            'data': {
                'title': fields.get('title', 'New Opportunity'),
                'company': fields.get('company'),
                'location': fields.get('location'),
                'salary': fields.get('salary'),
                'skills': fields.get('skills_required', '').split(','),
                'url': fields.get('url'),
                'timestamp': fields.get('created_at')
            }
        }
        
        await self.send(text_data=json.dumps(notification))
    
    @sync_to_async
    def get_user_skills(self):
        # Get user's skills for targeted notifications
        return ['python', 'django', 'javascript']  # Simplified
Printout: 
WebSocket Notification Sent:
{
  "type": "job_notification",
  "stream": "notifications:skill:python",
  "id": "1693920008000-0",
  "data": {
    "title": "Senior Python Developer",
    "company": "Tech Innovations",
    "location": "Seattle, WA",
    "salary": "95000",
    "skills": ["Python", "Django", "PostgreSQL"],
    "url": "/jobpost/jobs/6/",
    "timestamp": "2023-09-05T14:20:08Z"
  }
}

XGROUP CREATE - Create Consumer Group

Purpose: Creates a new consumer group for a stream, enabling multiple consumers to process entries.
Syntax: XGROUP CREATE key group ID|$ [MKSTREAM]
Parameters:
  • key - Stream name
  • group - Consumer group name
  • ID|$ - Starting position ('$' for new entries only)
  • MKSTREAM - Create the stream if it doesn't exist

Return: OK if successful
Django Example 1: Job Processing Pipeline Setup 
# management/commands/setup_job_pipeline.py
import redis
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Setup job processing consumer groups'
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        try:
            # Create consumer groups for different processing stages
            
            # 1. Application processing group
            redis_client.xgroup_create(
                'job_applications',
                'application_processors', 
                '$',  # Process only new applications
                mkstream=True
            )
            self.stdout.write("✅ Created application_processors group")
            
            # 2. Email notification group  
            redis_client.xgroup_create(
                'job_applications',
                'email_notifications',
                '0',  # Process from beginning for email backlog
                mkstream=True
            )
            self.stdout.write("✅ Created email_notifications group")
            
            # 3. Analytics group
            redis_client.xgroup_create(
                'job_applications', 
                'analytics_team',
                '0',  # Process all historical data
                mkstream=True
            )
            self.stdout.write("✅ Created analytics_team group")
            
            # 4. Performance monitoring group
            redis_client.xgroup_create(
                'performance_metrics',
                'monitoring_dashboard',
                '$',  # Only new metrics
                mkstream=True  
            )
            self.stdout.write("✅ Created monitoring_dashboard group")
            
            # 5. Priority application group (high salary jobs)
            redis_client.xgroup_create(
                'job_applications',
                'priority_processing',
                '$',
                mkstream=True
            )
            self.stdout.write("✅ Created priority_processing group")
            
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.SUCCESS(
                    "🎉 All consumer groups created successfully!\n"
                    "Available groups:\n"
                    "- application_processors (main processing)\n" 
                    "- email_notifications (automated emails)\n"
                    "- analytics_team (data analysis)\n"
                    "- monitoring_dashboard (real-time metrics)\n"
                    "- priority_processing (high-value applications)"
                )
            )
            
        except Exception as e:
            if "BUSYGROUP" in str(e):
                self.stdout.write("⚠️ Some groups already exist - skipping")
            else:
                self.stdout.write(f"❌ Error: {e}")
Printout: 
✅ Created application_processors group
✅ Created email_notifications group  
✅ Created analytics_team group
✅ Created monitoring_dashboard group
✅ Created priority_processing group

🎉 All consumer groups created successfully!
Available groups:
- application_processors (main processing)
- email_notifications (automated emails)
- analytics_team (data analysis)
- monitoring_dashboard (real-time metrics)
- priority_processing (high-value applications)
Django Example 2: Multi-Tenant Stream Groups 
# services/tenant_stream_setup.py
import redis
from django.db import models
from .models import Author

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class StreamGroupManager:
    @staticmethod
    def setup_company_groups():
        """Create consumer groups for each company"""
        
        companies = Author.objects.values_list('company', flat=True).distinct()
        
        for company in companies:
            company_key = company.replace(' ', '_').lower()
            
            try:
                # Create company-specific application processing group
                redis_client.xgroup_create(
                    f'company_applications:{company_key}',
                    f'{company_key}_hr_team',
                    '$',
                    mkstream=True
                )
                print(f"✅ Created HR group for {company}")
                
                # Create company-specific analytics group
                redis_client.xgroup_create(
                    f'company_applications:{company_key}',
                    f'{company_key}_analytics', 
                    '0',  # Process all historical data
                    mkstream=True
                )
                print(f"📊 Created analytics group for {company}")
                
                # Create company notification group
                redis_client.xgroup_create(
                    'notifications:job_posts',
                    f'{company_key}_notifications',
                    '$',
                    mkstream=True
                )
                print(f"🔔 Created notifications group for {company}")
                
            except Exception as e:
                if "BUSYGROUP" not in str(e):
                    print(f"❌ Error creating groups for {company}: {e}")
    
    @staticmethod
    def setup_skill_based_groups():
        """Create consumer groups for skill-based job matching"""
        
        from .models import Skills
        skills = Skills.objects.all()
        
        for skill in skills:
            skill_key = skill.name.lower().replace(' ', '_')
            
            try:
                # Create skill-specific processing group
                redis_client.xgroup_create(
                    f'notifications:skill:{skill_key}',
                    f'skill_matcher_{skill_key}',
                    '$',
                    mkstream=True
                )
                print(f"🎯 Created skill group for {skill.name}")
                
                # Create skill analytics group  
                redis_client.xgroup_create(
                    f'notifications:skill:{skill_key}',
                    f'skill_analytics_{skill_key}',
                    '0',
                    mkstream=True
                )
                print(f"📈 Created skill analytics for {skill.name}")
                
            except Exception as e:
                if "BUSYGROUP" not in str(e):
                    print(f"❌ Error for skill {skill.name}: {e}")
Printout: 
✅ Created HR group for ABC Corp
📊 Created analytics group for ABC Corp  
🔔 Created notifications group for ABC Corp
✅ Created HR group for XYZ Inc
📊 Created analytics group for XYZ Inc
🔔 Created notifications group for XYZ Inc

🎯 Created skill group for Python
📈 Created skill analytics for Python
🎯 Created skill group for JavaScript
📈 Created skill analytics for JavaScript
🎯 Created skill group for React
📈 Created skill analytics for React

XREADGROUP - Read from Consumer Group

Purpose: Read entries from a stream as part of a consumer group, providing load balancing and message delivery guarantees.
Syntax: XREADGROUP GROUP group consumer [COUNT count] [BLOCK milliseconds] [NOACK] STREAMS key [key ...] ID [ID ...]
Parameters:
  • GROUP group consumer - Consumer group and consumer name
  • COUNT - Maximum number of entries
  • BLOCK - Block waiting for entries
  • NOACK - Don't add to pending entries list
  • > - Read new entries not delivered to group

Return: Array of streams with entries assigned to this consumer
Django Example 1: Application Processing Worker 
# management/commands/process_applications.py
import redis
import time
import json
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.core.mail import send_mail
from .models import JobPost, User

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Process job applications from stream'
    
    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument('--consumer-name', default='worker-1', help='Consumer name')
        parser.add_argument('--batch-size', type=int, default=5, help='Batch size')
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        consumer_name = options['consumer_name']
        batch_size = options['batch_size']
        
        self.stdout.write(f"🚀 Starting application processor: {consumer_name}")
        
        while True:
            try:
                # Read from application processing group
                result = redis_client.xreadgroup(
                    'application_processors',  # group name
                    consumer_name,             # consumer name
                    streams={'job_applications': '>'},  # '>' means new entries
                    count=batch_size,
                    block=30000  # Block for 30 seconds
                )
                
                if result:
                    for stream_name, entries in result:
                        self.process_application_batch(entries, consumer_name)
                else:
                    self.stdout.write("⏰ No new applications - waiting...")
                    
            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                self.stdout.write("❌ Worker stopped")
                break
            except Exception as e:
                self.stdout.write(f"⚠️ Processing error: {e}")
                time.sleep(5)
    
    def process_application_batch(self, entries, consumer_name):
        self.stdout.write(f"📦 Processing batch of {len(entries)} applications...")
        
        for entry_id, fields in entries:
            try:
                # Process individual application
                job_id = fields.get('job_id')
                applicant_email = fields.get('applicant_email')
                
                self.stdout.write(
                    f"  🔍 Processing [{entry_id}]\n"
                    f"     Job: {fields.get('job_title')}\n"
                    f"     Applicant: {applicant_email}\n"
                    f"     Company: {fields.get('company')}\n"
                    f"     Consumer: {consumer_name}"
                )
                
                # Simulate processing steps
                self.validate_application(fields)
                self.score_application(fields)
                self.send_confirmation_email(fields)
                
                # Acknowledge successful processing
                redis_client.xack('job_applications', 'application_processors', entry_id)
                self.stdout.write(f"  ✅ Completed [{entry_id}]")
                
            except Exception as e:
                self.stdout.write(f"  ❌ Failed [{entry_id}]: {e}")
                # Leave in pending list for retry
    
    def validate_application(self, fields):
        # Application validation logic
        required_fields = ['job_id', 'applicant_email', 'job_title']
        for field in required_fields:
            if not fields.get(field):
                raise ValueError(f"Missing required field: {field}")
    
    def score_application(self, fields):
        # Simple scoring based on salary expectations
        salary = int(fields.get('salary', 0))
        score = min(salary // 1000, 100)  # Score out of 100
        return score
    
    def send_confirmation_email(self, fields):
        # Send confirmation email to applicant
        subject = f"Application Received: {fields.get('job_title')}"
        message = f"""
        Dear Applicant,
        
        We've received your application for {fields.get('job_title')} at {fields.get('company')}.
        
        Application details:
        - Position: {fields.get('job_title')}
        - Company: {fields.get('company')}  
        - Location: {fields.get('location')}
        - Salary: ${fields.get('salary')}
        
        We'll review your application and get back to you soon.
        
        Best regards,
        HR Team
        """
        
        # Simulate email sending
        self.stdout.write(f"  📧 Confirmation email sent to {fields.get('applicant_email')}")
Printout: 
🚀 Starting application processor: worker-1
📦 Processing batch of 3 applications...
  🔍 Processing [1693920010000-0]
     Job: Python Developer
     Applicant: jane.smith@example.com
     Company: ABC Corp
     Consumer: worker-1
  📧 Confirmation email sent to jane.smith@example.com
  ✅ Completed [1693920010000-0]

  🔍 Processing [1693920010001-0]  
     Job: Full Stack Developer
     Applicant: mike.johnson@example.com
     Company: 123 Industries
     Consumer: worker-1
  📧 Confirmation email sent to mike.johnson@example.com
  ✅ Completed [1693920010001-0]
Django Example 2: Analytics Processing Worker 
# management/commands/analytics_processor.py
import redis
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.utils import timezone
from collections import defaultdict, Counter
import json

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Process applications for analytics and insights'
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        consumer_name = f"analytics-worker-{timezone.now().strftime('%H%M%S')}"
        
        self.stdout.write(f"📊 Starting analytics processor: {consumer_name}")
        
        # Analytics data structures
        daily_stats = defaultdict(int)
        company_stats = defaultdict(int)
        skill_demand = Counter()
        salary_ranges = []
        
        while True:
            try:
                result = redis_client.xreadgroup(
                    'analytics_team',
                    consumer_name,
                    streams={'job_applications': '>'},
                    count=10,
                    block=15000
                )
                
                if result:
                    for stream_name, entries in result:
                        for entry_id, fields in entries:
                            self.process_for_analytics(
                                entry_id, fields, daily_stats, 
                                company_stats, skill_demand, salary_ranges
                            )
                            
                    # Generate periodic reports
                    self.generate_analytics_report(
                        daily_stats, company_stats, skill_demand, salary_ranges
                    )
                        
            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                self.stdout.write("📈 Analytics processor stopped")
                break
            except Exception as e:
                self.stdout.write(f"⚠️ Analytics error: {e}")
    
    def process_for_analytics(self, entry_id, fields, daily_stats, 
                            company_stats, skill_demand, salary_ranges):
        try:
            # Extract analytics data
            company = fields.get('company', 'unknown')
            salary = int(fields.get('salary', 0))
            timestamp = fields.get('timestamp', '')
            date = timestamp.split('T')[0] if timestamp else 'unknown'
            
            # Update statistics
            daily_stats[date] += 1
            company_stats[company] += 1
            salary_ranges.append(salary)
            
            # Track skills demand (if available in application)
            if 'skills' in fields:
                skills = fields['skills'].split(',')
                for skill in skills:
                    skill_demand[skill.strip()] += 1
            
            # Store processed analytics entry
            redis_client.xadd(
                'analytics_processed',
                {
                    'original_entry_id': entry_id,
                    'company': company,
                    'salary': str(salary),
                    'date': date,
                    'processing_time': timezone.now().isoformat(),
                    'processor': 'analytics_team'
                }
            )
            
            # Acknowledge processing
            redis_client.xack('job_applications', 'analytics_team', entry_id)
            
            self.stdout.write(f"📈 Analyzed [{entry_id}] - {company}: ${salary}")
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.stdout.write(f"❌ Analytics error for [{entry_id}]: {e}")
    
    def generate_analytics_report(self, daily_stats, company_stats, 
                                skill_demand, salary_ranges):
        if not salary_ranges:
            return
            
        # Calculate salary statistics
        avg_salary = sum(salary_ranges) / len(salary_ranges)
        min_salary = min(salary_ranges)
        max_salary = max(salary_ranges)
        
        report = {
            'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat(),
            'total_applications': len(salary_ranges),
            'salary_stats': {
                'average': f"${avg_salary:,.2f}",
                'min': f"${min_salary:,}",
                'max': f"${max_salary:,}"
            },
            'top_companies': dict(company_stats.most_common(5)),
            'daily_breakdown': dict(daily_stats),
            'skill_demand': dict(skill_demand.most_common(10))
        }
        
        # Store report in Redis
        redis_client.set(
            f'analytics_report:{timezone.now().date()}',
            json.dumps(report),
            ex=86400  # Expire after 1 day
        )
        
        self.stdout.write(
            f"\n📊 ANALYTICS REPORT\n"
            f"   Total Applications: {report['total_applications']}\n"
            f"   Average Salary: {report['salary_stats']['average']}\n"
            f"   Top Company: {list(company_stats.keys())[0] if company_stats else 'N/A'}\n"
            f"   Most Demanded Skill: {skill_demand.most_common(1)[0][0] if skill_demand else 'N/A'}\n"
        )
Printout: 
📊 Starting analytics processor: analytics-worker-142005
📈 Analyzed [1693920011000-0] - ABC Corp: $80000
📈 Analyzed [1693920011001-0] - XYZ Inc: $75000  
📈 Analyzed [1693920011002-0] - Tech Solutions: $90000

📊 ANALYTICS REPORT
   Total Applications: 3
   Average Salary: $81,666.67
   Top Company: ABC Corp
   Most Demanded Skill: Python

XACK - Acknowledge Entry Processing

Purpose: Acknowledge that entries have been successfully processed by a consumer, removing them from the pending list.
Syntax: XACK key group ID [ID ...]
Parameters:
  • key - Stream name
  • group - Consumer group name
  • ID - Entry ID(s) to acknowledge

Return: Number of entries acknowledged
Django Example 1: Reliable Email Processing 
# services/email_processor.py
import redis
from django.core.mail import send_mail
from django.template.loader import render_to_string
from django.conf import settings
import logging

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class EmailProcessorService:
    def __init__(self, consumer_name='email-processor'):
        self.consumer_name = consumer_name
        self.group_name = 'email_notifications'
        self.stream_name = 'job_applications'
    
    def process_email_queue(self):
        """Process emails with reliable acknowledgment"""
        
        while True:
            try:
                # Read pending emails first (failed previous attempts)
                pending_result = redis_client.xreadgroup(
                    self.group_name,
                    self.consumer_name,
                    streams={self.stream_name: '0'},  # '0' gets pending entries
                    count=5
                )
                
                if pending_result:
                    logger.info(f"🔄 Processing {len(pending_result[0][1])} pending emails")
                    self.process_email_batch(pending_result[0][1], is_retry=True)
                
                # Read new entries
                new_result = redis_client.xreadgroup(
                    self.group_name, 
                    self.consumer_name,
                    streams={self.stream_name: '>'},  # '>' gets new entries
                    count=10,
                    block=20000
                )
                
                if new_result:
                    logger.info(f"📧 Processing {len(new_result[0][1])} new emails")
                    self.process_email_batch(new_result[0][1], is_retry=False)
                    
            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                logger.info("📧 Email processor stopped")
                break
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"⚠️ Email processing error: {e}")
    
    def process_email_batch(self, entries, is_retry=False):
        """Process batch of email entries with proper acknowledgment"""
        
        for entry_id, fields in entries:
            try:
                # Extract email details
                recipient_email = fields.get('applicant_email')
                job_title = fields.get('job_title')
                company = fields.get('company')
                applicant_name = fields.get('applicant_name', 'Applicant')
                
                if not recipient_email or not job_title:
                    logger.warning(f"⚠️ Missing email data in [{entry_id}]")
                    continue
                
                # Send confirmation email
                success = self.send_application_confirmation(
                    recipient_email, job_title, company, applicant_name, is_retry
                )
                
                if success:
                    # Acknowledge successful email sending
                    ack_result = redis_client.xack(
                        self.stream_name, 
                        self.group_name, 
                        entry_id
                    )
                    
                    if ack_result:
                        logger.info(f"✅ Email sent and acknowledged [{entry_id}]")
                        
                        # Store successful email log
                        redis_client.xadd(
                            'email_sent_log',
                            {
                                'original_entry_id': entry_id,
                                'recipient': recipient_email,
                                'job_title': job_title,
                                'company': company,
                                'retry_attempt': 'yes' if is_retry else 'no',
                                'sent_at': timezone.now().isoformat(),
                                'processor': self.consumer_name
                            }
                        )
                    else:
                        logger.error(f"❌ Failed to acknowledge [{entry_id}]")
                else:
                    logger.error(f"📧 Email sending failed for [{entry_id}] - left in pending")
                    
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"❌ Email processing error [{entry_id}]: {e}")
                # Don't acknowledge on error - entry stays in pending list
    
    def send_application_confirmation(self, recipient_email, job_title, 
                                    company, applicant_name, is_retry):
        """Send application confirmation email"""
        try:
            subject = f"Application Received: {job_title} at {company}"
            
            # Use Django template for professional email
            email_context = {
                'applicant_name': applicant_name,
                'job_title': job_title,
                'company': company,
                'application_date': timezone.now().date(),
                'is_retry': is_retry
            }
            
            html_message = render_to_string(
                'emails/application_confirmation.html', 
                email_context
            )
            plain_message = render_to_string(
                'emails/application_confirmation.txt',
                email_context  
            )
            
            send_mail(
                subject=subject,
                message=plain_message,
                from_email=settings.DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL,
                recipient_list=[recipient_email],
                html_message=html_message,
                fail_silently=False
            )
            
            retry_note = " (RETRY)" if is_retry else ""
            logger.info(f"📧 Email sent to {recipient_email}{retry_note}")
            return True
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"📧 Email sending failed to {recipient_email}: {e}")
            return False
Printout: 
🔄 Processing 2 pending emails
📧 Email sent to jane.smith@example.com (RETRY)
✅ Email sent and acknowledged [1693920012000-0]
📧 Email sent to mike.johnson@example.com (RETRY)  
✅ Email sent and acknowledged [1693920012001-0]

📧 Processing 3 new emails
📧 Email sent to sarah.davis@example.com
✅ Email sent and acknowledged [1693920013000-0]
📧 Email sent to david.wilson@example.com
✅ Email sent and acknowledged [1693920013001-0]

Email Processing Summary:
- Pending emails processed: 2/2
- New emails processed: 3/3  
- Acknowledgments: 5/5
- Failed emails: 0
Django Example 2: Priority Processing with Acknowledgment 
# management/commands/priority_processor.py
import redis
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.core.mail import send_mail
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from django.utils import timezone
from .models import JobPost, Author

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Process high-priority applications (high salary/urgent)'
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        consumer_name = 'priority-processor-1'
        group_name = 'priority_processing'
        
        self.stdout.write(f"⚡ Starting priority processor: {consumer_name}")
        
        processed_count = 0
        acknowledged_count = 0
        
        while True:
            try:
                result = redis_client.xreadgroup(
                    group_name,
                    consumer_name,
                    streams={'job_applications': '>'},
                    count=3,  # Small batches for priority processing
                    block=10000
                )
                
                if result:
                    for stream_name, entries in result:
                        for entry_id, fields in entries:
                            processed_count += 1
                            
                            if self.is_priority_application(fields):
                                success = self.process_priority_application(entry_id, fields)
                                
                                if success:
                                    # Acknowledge only after successful processing
                                    ack_count = redis_client.xack(
                                        'job_applications',
                                        group_name,
                                        entry_id
                                    )
                                    
                                    if ack_count > 0:
                                        acknowledged_count += 1
                                        self.stdout.write(
                                            f"⚡ Priority application processed [{entry_id}] "
                                            f"({acknowledged_count}/{processed_count})"
                                        )
                                    else:
                                        self.stdout.write(f"⚠️ Ack failed for [{entry_id}]")
                                else:
                                    self.stdout.write(f"❌ Processing failed [{entry_id}] - not acknowledged")
                            else:
                                # Not priority - acknowledge without special processing  
                                redis_client.xack('job_applications', group_name, entry_id)
                                acknowledged_count += 1
                                
            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                self.stdout.write(
                    f"⚡ Priority processor stopped\n"
                    f"   Processed: {processed_count}\n" 
                    f"   Acknowledged: {acknowledged_count}"
                )
                break
            except Exception as e:
                self.stdout.write(f"⚠️ Priority processing error: {e}")
    
    def is_priority_application(self, fields):
        """Determine if application requires priority processing"""
        try:
            salary = int(fields.get('salary', 0))
            company = fields.get('company', '').lower()
            
            # Priority criteria
            high_salary = salary >= 85000
            premium_company = any(keyword in company for keyword in ['tech', 'google', 'microsoft', 'amazon'])
            urgent_keywords = any(keyword in fields.get('job_title', '').lower() 
                                for keyword in ['senior', 'lead', 'principal', 'director'])
            
            return high_salary or premium_company or urgent_keywords
            
        except ValueError:
            return False
    
    def process_priority_application(self, entry_id, fields):
        """Process priority applications with special handling"""
        try:
            job_title = fields.get('job_title')
            company = fields.get('company')  
            applicant_email = fields.get('applicant_email')
            salary = fields.get('salary')
            
            self.stdout.write(
                f"🎯 PRIORITY APPLICATION [{entry_id}]\n"
                f"   Job: {job_title}\n"
                f"   Company: {company}\n" 
                f"   Salary: ${salary}\n"
                f"   Applicant: {applicant_email}"
            )
            
            # 1. Send immediate notification to hiring manager
            self.notify_hiring_manager(fields)
            
            # 2. Flag in database for expedited review
            self.flag_for_expedited_review(fields)
            
            # 3. Add to priority processing log
            redis_client.xadd(
                'priority_applications_log',
                {
                    'original_entry_id': entry_id,
                    'job_title': job_title,
                    'company': company,
                    'salary': salary,
                    'applicant_email': applicant_email,
                    'priority_reason': self.get_priority_reason(fields),
                    'processed_at': timezone.now().isoformat(),
                    'processor': 'priority-processor-1',
                    'status': 'completed'
                }
            )
            
            return True
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.stdout.write(f"❌ Priority processing failed: {e}")
            return False
    
    def notify_hiring_manager(self, fields):
        """Send immediate notification to hiring manager"""
        subject = f"🚨 Priority Application: {fields.get('job_title')}"
        message = f"""
        Priority application received requiring immediate attention:
        
        Position: {fields.get('job_title')}
        Company: {fields.get('company')}
        Salary: ${fields.get('salary')}
        Applicant: {fields.get('applicant_email')}
        Location: {fields.get('location')}
        
        Please review this application as soon as possible.
        """
        
        # Send to hiring manager email
        hiring_manager_email = f"hiring@{fields.get('company', 'company').lower().replace(' ', '')}.com"
        
        self.stdout.write(f"📧 Priority notification sent to {hiring_manager_email}")
    
    def flag_for_expedited_review(self, fields):
        """Flag application in database for expedited processing"""
        self.stdout.write("🚩 Application flagged for expedited review")
    
    def get_priority_reason(self, fields):
        """Get reason why application was marked as priority"""
        reasons = []
        
        try:
            salary = int(fields.get('salary', 0))
            if salary >= 85000:
                reasons.append(f"high_salary_${salary}")
        except ValueError:
            pass
            
        company = fields.get('company', '').lower()
        if any(keyword in company for keyword in ['tech', 'google', 'microsoft', 'amazon']):
            reasons.append("premium_company")
            
        job_title = fields.get('job_title', '').lower()
        if any(keyword in job_title for keyword in ['senior', 'lead', 'principal', 'director']):
            reasons.append("senior_position")
        
        return ','.join(reasons) if reasons else 'unknown'
Printout: 
⚡ Starting priority processor: priority-processor-1

   Job: Senior Python Developer  
   Company: Tech Innovations
   Salary: $95000
   Applicant: expert.dev@example.com
📧 Priority notification sent to hiring@techinnovations.com
🚩 Application flagged for expedited review
⚡ Priority application processed [1693920014000-0] (1/1)

🎯 PRIORITY APPLICATION [1693920014001-0]
   Job: Data Science Director
   Company: Amazon Corp
   Salary: $120000
   Applicant: data.scientist@example.com
📧 Priority notification sent to hiring@amazoncorp.com
🚩 Application flagged for expedited review  
⚡ Priority application processed [1693920014001-0] (2/2)

XLEN - Get Stream Length

Purpose: Returns the number of entries in a stream.
Syntax: XLEN key
Parameters:
  • key - Stream name

Return: Number of entries in the stream
Django Example: Stream Monitoring Dashboard 
# views.py - Stream monitoring dashboard
import redis
from django.shortcuts import render
from django.http import JsonResponse
from django.utils import timezone

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

def stream_dashboard(request):
    """Dashboard showing stream statistics and health"""
    
    # Get lengths of all monitored streams
    stream_stats = {}
    
    streams_to_monitor = [
        'job_applications',
        'user_activity:1',
        'performance_metrics', 
        'notifications:job_posts',
        'email_sent_log',
        'priority_applications_log',
        'analytics_processed'
    ]
    
    for stream_name in streams_to_monitor:
        try:
            length = redis_client.xlen(stream_name)
            stream_stats[stream_name] = {
                'length': length,
                'status': 'healthy' if length < 10000 else 'warning' if length < 50000 else 'critical'
            }
        except Exception as e:
            stream_stats[stream_name] = {
                'length': 0,
                'status': 'error',
                'error': str(e)
            }
    
    # Calculate totals and health
    total_entries = sum(stats['length'] for stats in stream_stats.values() if 'error' not in stats)
    healthy_streams = sum(1 for stats in stream_stats.values() if stats.get('status') == 'healthy')
    total_streams = len(stream_stats)
    
    dashboard_data = {
        'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat(),
        'streams': stream_stats,
        'summary': {
            'total_entries': total_entries,
            'healthy_streams': healthy_streams,
            'total_streams': total_streams,
            'health_percentage': round((healthy_streams / total_streams) * 100, 1) if total_streams > 0 else 0
        }
    }
    
    if request.headers.get('Accept') == 'application/json':
        return JsonResponse(dashboard_data)
    
    return render(request, 'dashboard/streams.html', {
        'dashboard_data': dashboard_data
    })
Printout: 
Stream Dashboard Data:
{
  "timestamp": "2023-09-05T14:20:15Z",
  "streams": {
    "job_applications": {"length": 1247, "status": "healthy"},
    "user_activity:1": {"length": 523, "status": "healthy"},
    "performance_metrics": {"length": 8934, "status": "healthy"},
    "notifications:job_posts": {"length": 892, "status": "healthy"},
    "email_sent_log": {"length": 1180, "status": "healthy"},
    "priority_applications_log": {"length": 67, "status": "healthy"},
    "analytics_processed": {"length": 1247, "status": "healthy"}
  },
  "summary": {
    "total_entries": 14090,
    "healthy_streams": 7,
    "total_streams": 7,
    "health_percentage": 100.0
  }
}
